mcqs.question: What is ambiguity in a grammar?

options.text: A situation where a single string can be parsed in more than one way
A situation where a single string can be parsed in only one way
A situation where a grammar is not well-defined
A situation where a language is not well-defined

mcqs.explanation: Ambiguity in a grammar refers to the situation where a single string can be parsed in more than one way, resulting in multiple parse trees.

mcqs.question: What are the two types of ambiguity?

options.text: Inherent Ambiguity and Grammar Ambiguity
Inherent Ambiguity and Left Recursion
Grammar Ambiguity and Left Recursion
Inherent Ambiguity and Right Recursion

mcqs.explanation: There are two types of ambiguity: Inherent Ambiguity and Grammar Ambiguity.

mcqs.question: What is left recursion?

options.text: A situation where a production has a common prefix with another production
A situation where a production has a common suffix with another production
A situation where a production calls itself recursively
A situation where a production does not call itself recursively

mcqs.explanation: Left recursion occurs when a production calls itself recursively.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of left factoring?

options.text: To eliminate left recursion
To factor out common prefixes
To factor out common suffixes
To resolve ambiguity

mcqs.explanation: Left factoring is used to factor out common prefixes from two or more productions.

mcqs.question: What is the advantage of eliminating left recursion?

options.text: It can be complex to apply
It may not always resolve ambiguity
It always resolves ambiguity
It is easy to apply

mcqs.explanation: Eliminating left recursion always resolves ambiguity.
